responseText:
Summary of Feb 27 – Mar 8, 2026 (10‑day Rivian activity)

High‑level:
- 70 drives
- 1,072 miles driven
- 17 charging sessions
- ~670 kWh added
- Travel across CT, MA, NH, and VT with several multi‑state trips

Narrative of the period

Feb 27 — Departure day  
The Rivian started the morning with short local trips around Cornwall, CT. Mid‑day it charged twice at home, preparing for a longer drive. In the evening the real movement began: a 93.6‑mile drive from Cornwall to Bennington, VT, followed by a fast DC charge. Shortly after, the trip continued another 58 miles to Ludlow, VT, arriving late evening.

Feb 28 — Local Vermont movement  
The vehicle stayed around the Ludlow/Plymouth area with a few short trips (about 6–7 miles each). Overnight Level‑2 charging brought the battery from the low 40s to about 85%.

Mar 1 — Return toward Connecticut  
Small errands around Ludlow occurred in the morning. Mid‑afternoon the Rivian began a major return leg:  
- 123.5 miles from Ludlow → Canaan, CT.  
Battery arrived low (~17%), prompting a fast charge in North Canaan.  
The evening continued with several short drives between Cornwall and Kent.

Mar 2 — Berkshire / Southern Vermont loop  
Morning travel included a 34‑mile drive from Cornwall → Egremont, MA.  
After a short stop in Great Barrington, the Rivian headed north again on a longer 71.9‑mile drive to Dover, VT.  
An overnight charge there restored the battery to ~85%.

Mar 3 — Long northern travel day  
This was one of the busiest driving days:
- Local Dover errands
- 19 miles to Dummerston, VT
- 53.5 miles to West Lebanon, NH (fast charge stop)
- 77.2 miles to Stowe, VT

This day stitched together southern Vermont, New Hampshire, and northern Vermont in a continuous travel chain.

Mar 4 — Vermont to Massachusetts run  
Morning driving around Stowe and Waterbury.  
Later a sequence of longer segments:
- Waterbury → Montpelier  
- Montpelier → Lebanon, NH (fast charge)  
- Lebanon → Holyoke, MA (114.5 miles)

A short evening drive placed the vehicle in Springfield, MA.

Mar 5 — Return home to Cornwall  
Early morning:
- Springfield → Windsor Locks, CT  
- Windsor Locks → Torrington, CT  
- Torrington → Cornwall

After arriving back in Cornwall, the rest of the day consisted of short local trips around Cream Hill Rd and Kent Rd, followed by home charging sessions that evening and overnight.

Mar 6–8 — Mostly stationary with charging  
Driving activity drops sharply after the travel period. Charging sessions at home continue (bringing the battery back to ~85%), with only minimal movement recorded.

Notable trips
- Feb 27: Cornwall, CT → Bennington, VT (93.6 mi) → Ludlow, VT (58.1 mi)
- Mar 1: Ludlow, VT → Canaan, CT (123.5 mi)
- Mar 3: Dummerston, VT → West Lebanon, NH → Stowe, VT chain
- Mar 4: Lebanon, NH → Holyoke, MA (114.5 mi)
- Mar 5: Springfield, MA → Cornwall, CT return sequence

Overall story
Over these ten days the Rivian completed a multi‑leg regional tour centered on Vermont ski‑area towns and New England corridors. It departed Cornwall for southern Vermont (Bennington/Ludlow), returned briefly to Connecticut, then went back north through the Berkshires and Vermont again—eventually reaching Stowe. From there it worked its way south through New Hampshire and Massachusetts before finally returning to Cornwall on March 5, after which activity became mostly local.

If you want, I can also:
- map the entire route visually,
- calculate total road‑trip mileage vs local mileage,
- or estimate total charging cost and DC fast‑charging vs home charging usage for this trip.

transcript.text: look through my real data and summarize the 10 days of the rivians activity between february 27 and march 8 2026. if there were any notable trips, create a narrative of the time frame. today is mar 8 2026 and make sure that you include full 10 days, so if you don't have any drives and chargers in february its obviously false.
